meta_title: Impact Windows in Sheridan, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact windows in Sheridan, FL. ESW & CWI brands, in-house crews, financing from $87/mo. Protect your home today.
h1: Impact Windows in Sheridan, FL — Serious Storm Protection for a Solid Community
intro:
Sheridan may sit about four miles inland from the Atlantic shoreline, but that distance offers little comfort when a major hurricane is bearing down on Broward County. Homeowners throughout this established community know all too well that South Florida's tropical weather doesn't respect zip codes. With a 170 mph wind zone designation and a track record that includes direct impacts from Hurricane Andrew, Wilma, and Irma, properties in and around Sheridan face real, recurring storm threats that demand real, code-compliant protection. Impact windows are no longer a luxury upgrade here — they're a smart, lasting investment that safeguards your family, your belongings, and your home's long-term value.

The neighborhood surrounding Sheridan Park is a mix of mid-century ranch homes and updated single-family residences, many of which were built before 1994 — a critical benchmark in Florida construction history. In fact, roughly half of the homes in this area predate the sweeping building code reforms that followed Hurricane Andrew's catastrophic landfall in 1992. That means a significant portion of Sheridan's housing stock was designed to standards that modern meteorologists and structural engineers now consider inadequate for the storm intensity South Florida regularly faces. For homeowners in these older properties, upgrading to impact-resistant windows isn't just a quality-of-life improvement; it's a structural necessity that can mean the difference between minor cleanup and devastating loss.

With average home values hovering around $350,000, a Sheridan residence represents a major financial asset worth protecting. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing — known throughout Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties as "The Kings of Service" — specializes in helping middle-income homeowners make this critical upgrade without breaking the bank. From initial consultation and permit filing with the Broward County Building Division to the final installation by our own in-house crews, we manage every step of the process. Whether you live steps from Sheridan Park or near the border with neighboring Hollywood, Pembroke Pines, or Davie, we bring over 60 years of combined installation experience directly to your doorstep.

section_why.h2: Why Sheridan Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
section_why.body:
Choosing an impact window company in South Florida means wading through a crowded market full of salespeople who outsource their labor, push inferior products, and disappear after the deposit clears. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing operates differently. We are a fully in-house operation, meaning every technician who arrives at your Sheridan home is a trained, vetted member of our own team — not a subcontractor hired off a list. This single distinction sets us apart from most competitors in Broward County, and it directly affects the quality and accountability of your installation.

Our team brings over 60 years of combined experience installing impact windows across hundreds of South Florida homes. We've completed more than 500 residential projects throughout Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, and we've earned an A+ rating from the Better Business Bureau by doing things the right way every time. We understand the specific quirks of Broward County permitting, the nuances of installing in older pre-1994 homes with non-standard framing, and the weather patterns that make certain window configurations more effective for properties like those near Sheridan Park. That local knowledge isn't something you can acquire from a national chain — it comes from years of working in these specific communities.

We're also fully licensed and insured, which protects you legally and financially throughout the project. When we pull permits through the Broward County Building Division, we handle all the paperwork, coordination, and inspection scheduling. You won't be left chasing down an inspector or trying to understand code language on your own. Our job is to make this process as smooth as possible for you, from the first phone call to the final walkthrough.

For Sheridan homeowners who want premium results without premium stress, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is the clear choice. We combine product excellence, skilled craftsmanship, and genuine customer service in a way that has made us one of the most trusted names in South Florida home improvement.

section_history.h2: Sheridan's Storm History Makes a Strong Case for Impact Windows
section_history.body:
Broward County has been struck by nine significant storms since 1990, and Sheridan has felt the effects of the most destructive ones. Hurricane Andrew in August 1992 remains the defining storm of the modern South Florida era — a Category 5 monster that obliterated entire neighborhoods in Miami-Dade and sent shockwaves of destruction northward into Broward. While Andrew's core tracked south of Sheridan, the storm's outer bands still delivered damaging winds and flying debris that exposed just how vulnerable the area's older housing stock truly was. The aftermath of Andrew is what triggered Florida's comprehensive building code overhaul, yet half of Sheridan's homes were already built before those reforms took effect.

Hurricane Wilma in October 2005 delivered a more direct blow. Wilma tracked directly across South Florida from southwest to northeast, subjecting Broward County to sustained hurricane-force winds for hours. The storm caused widespread roof damage, shattered unprotected windows, and left hundreds of thousands without power for weeks. Homeowners who had invested in impact windows prior to Wilma reported dramatically less interior damage, lower repair costs, and faster recovery timelines compared to neighbors relying on traditional glass and plywood. Hurricane Irma in 2017 reinforced the lesson: properties with impact-rated openings consistently perform better in high-wind events.

These aren't abstract statistics. They represent real damage to real homes in and around the Sheridan area. With storm frequency showing no signs of decreasing and climate trends pointing toward more intense Atlantic hurricane seasons, the question for Sheridan homeowners isn't whether to upgrade to impact windows — it's how soon.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Windows in Sheridan and Broward County
section_code.body:
Sheridan sits within Broward County's jurisdiction and falls under Florida Building Code (FBC) requirements that mandate hurricane-rated products for all exterior openings. While the Sheridan area is not classified within the High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a designation that applies to Miami-Dade and Broward coastal municipalities — it still carries a 170 mph design wind speed requirement. This means any window product installed must be tested and rated to withstand those wind loads, along with the missile impact and cyclic pressure tests required by the FBC. Not every product on the market qualifies, which is why working with a knowledgeable installer matters.

All impact window installations in Sheridan require permits pulled through the Broward County Building Division. This process involves submitting product approval documentation, installation drawings, and proof of contractor licensing before work can begin. After installation, a licensed inspector must verify that the work meets code before the permit is closed. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles this entire process in-house — our team prepares and submits the permit application, coordinates the inspection, and ensures every product we install carries the appropriate Florida Product Approval (FPA) number. You never have to navigate Broward County's building department on your own.

It's worth noting that for homes built before 1994 in Sheridan, additional considerations may apply. Older framing systems, non-standard rough opening dimensions, and outdated structural anchoring methods can complicate installation if not addressed properly. Our experienced crews are trained to identify and resolve these issues during the project scope phase, ensuring your installation is both code-compliant and structurally sound for decades to come.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Windows: The Products Premier Installs in Sheridan Homes
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two industry-leading product lines for residential impact window projects in Sheridan: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both brands are manufactured specifically for South Florida's demanding climate and carry the product approvals required for installation under Florida Building Code. When you choose Premier, you're not getting a generic box-store product or a brand with an unproven track record — you're getting windows engineered for the specific wind loads and storm conditions that Broward County homeowners face.

ESW impact windows feature laminated safety glass, which holds together even when the glass itself is struck or cracked by airborne debris. Unlike tempered glass, which shatters into fragments, laminated glass maintains a barrier between the storm outside and your living space inside. This is the same technology used in automotive windshields, and it's what gives impact windows their defining advantage over traditional single-pane or even double-pane alternatives. ESW frames are built with multi-chamber construction, which enhances structural rigidity and provides superior thermal insulation — an important benefit for Sheridan homeowners looking to reduce year-round energy costs, not just prepare for storm season.

CWI products complement the ESW lineup with additional configuration options for single-family homes throughout Sheridan. Both brands incorporate Low-E (low-emissivity) glass coatings that reflect infrared heat while allowing natural light to pass through. In a South Florida climate where air conditioning runs for the majority of the year, Low-E coatings can produce meaningful reductions in monthly utility bills. Between storm protection, noise reduction, UV filtering, and energy efficiency, ESW and CWI impact windows deliver a full-spectrum upgrade that enhances your home on every level.

section_cost.h2: What Impact Windows Cost in Sheridan — and How to Finance Them Affordably
section_cost.body:
For a home valued around $350,000 in Sheridan, the cost of a full impact window replacement typically ranges depending on the number of openings, window sizes, and any structural modifications required for older pre-1994 homes. Most residential projects in this price range involve an investment that, while significant upfront, delivers measurable financial returns through insurance savings, energy cost reductions, and increased resale value. Impact windows are consistently cited by South Florida real estate professionals as one of the highest-ROI home improvements a homeowner can make, particularly in hurricane-prone markets like Broward County.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes this investment accessible through flexible financing options designed for middle-income homeowners. Our financing programs start as low as $87 per month, with $0 down options available for qualified applicants. This means you can have professionally installed, code-compliant impact windows in your Sheridan home without depleting your savings or waiting years to accumulate the full cost. The monthly payment is often offset — or even exceeded — by the insurance premium reductions that many Broward County homeowners see after installation, making the upgrade essentially self-funding over time.

Beyond monthly savings, consider the avoided cost of storm damage. A single hurricane event can cause tens of thousands of dollars in interior damage when windows fail — damaged furniture, flooring, drywall, electronics, and personal belongings add up quickly. Impact windows significantly reduce the likelihood of that scenario, which means the investment also functions as a form of self-insurance. When you factor in the combination of financing availability, insurance discounts, energy savings, and damage prevention, the financial case for impact windows in Sheridan is remarkably strong.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings for Sheridan Homeowners After Impact Window Installation
section_insurance.body:
One of the most compelling financial arguments for impact windows in Sheridan is the potential for significant homeowners insurance premium reductions. Florida insurers are required to offer discounts for wind mitigation features, and impact-rated windows are among the most heavily credited upgrades under the state's wind mitigation inspection framework. After installation, homeowners can schedule a wind mitigation inspection — typically performed by a licensed inspector — and submit the resulting report to their insurance carrier. Many Broward County homeowners report annual premium reductions of several hundred dollars or more following this process, with some properties in high-risk categories seeing even larger savings.

Common homeowners insurance carriers operating in the Broward County market — including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property, Heritage, and others — all recognize wind mitigation credits. The specific discount varies by carrier, policy type, and the percentage of openings protected, but fully protected homes (all openings covered with impact-rated products) typically qualify for the maximum available credits. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs windows that meet the product approval standards required to qualify for these credits, and we can provide the documentation your inspector will need to complete the wind mitigation report.

It's also worth considering the broader insurance landscape in Florida, where carriers have been restricting coverage, raising premiums, and in some cases exiting the market entirely due to storm loss exposure. Homeowners with documented wind mitigation features are generally viewed as lower-risk policyholders, which can also improve your options when shopping for or renewing coverage. In an increasingly challenging insurance environment, impact windows aren't just a safety feature — they're a strategic asset that keeps your home insurable and your premiums manageable for years to come.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact windows in Sheridan if I'm not right on the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Being four miles inland doesn't reduce your exposure to hurricane-force winds — it just changes the storm surge risk. Sheridan sits in a 170 mph wind zone, and Broward County has been directly impacted by major storms including Wilma and Irma. High winds, flying debris, and pressure differentials during a hurricane pose the same threat to inland homes as they do to coastal properties. Impact windows protect against all of these hazards regardless of your distance from the water.

faqs.q: How much do impact windows cost in Sheridan, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Project costs vary based on the number of windows, sizes, and any modifications needed for older homes. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options for qualified homeowners. Given that many Sheridan homeowners see annual insurance savings of several hundred dollars after installation, the financing cost is often partially or fully offset by reduced premiums, making impact windows a financially smart upgrade even for budget-conscious homeowners.

faqs.q: What permits are required for impact window installation in Sheridan?
faqs.a: All impact window installations in Sheridan require permits through the Broward County Building Division. The permit application must include product approval documentation, installation drawings, and licensed contractor credentials. After installation, a county inspector must verify code compliance before the permit is closed. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process in-house — we prepare and submit all paperwork, coordinate inspections, and ensure your project meets Florida Building Code requirements from start to finish.

faqs.q: How long does it take to install impact windows in a typical Sheridan home?
faqs.a: For most single-family homes in Sheridan, the physical installation is typically completed in one to two days, depending on the number of openings and any structural complexities. The overall project timeline — from signed contract to completed inspection — generally spans four to six weeks, with much of that time spent on permitting and product lead times. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing keeps you informed throughout the process and works efficiently to minimize disruption to your household.

faqs.q: Will impact windows actually lower my homeowners insurance in Broward County?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes. Florida law requires insurers to offer wind mitigation discounts, and impact-rated windows are one of the most valuable credits available. After your installation, a licensed wind mitigation inspector can document your protected openings, and you submit that report to your carrier for a rate adjustment. Many Broward County homeowners see annual savings of several hundred dollars or more. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs products with the required Florida Product Approvals and provides documentation to support your wind mitigation inspection.
